task_completion.description: Create the inner and outer arcs of the omega strap centered at the origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D sketch view, two concentric semi-circles (arcs) are visible, centered at the origin (0,0) with their endpoints resting on the horizontal axis.

task_completion.description: Draw the symmetric mounting flanges on each side of the arcs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Straight line segments forming flanges are attached to the ends of the arcs on both sides. Each flange consists of a bottom horizontal line, a vertical outer line, and a top horizontal line connecting back to the outer arc, forming a closed profile.

task_completion.description: Apply the required dimensional constraints to the 2D profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension labels are visible on the sketch: R20 (or diameter 40) for the inner arc, R25 (or diameter 50) for the outer arc, length 20 for the horizontal flange edges, and length 5 for the vertical outer edges.

task_completion.description: Extrude the fully-constrained 2D profile into a 3D solid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D solid model of the omega strap is visible in the viewport. The property panel or a visible dimension constraint indicates an extrusion depth/width of 30mm.

task_completion.description: Save the model to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar or a save dialog displays the filename 'pipe_strap.slvs', confirming the model has been saved.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application interface (toolbars, property browser, 3D viewport) being actively used, rather than a text editor or terminal writing the .slvs file directly.

integrity.description: The model was built step-by-step, not copied or loaded from a pre-existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows a progression from an empty sketch to drawing arcs, adding lines, applying dimensions, and finally extruding, proving the model was synthetically created.

integrity.description: The 3D model is genuinely rendered in the CAD software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The model is displayed within the SolveSpace viewport with its characteristic grid, coordinate axes, and UI elements, rather than being an image opened in a picture viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/pipe_strap.slvs. The model must contain exactly 2 arcs and 8 lines in its 2D profile. The required dimensions are: inner arc radius = 20.0mm, outer arc radius = 25.0mm, flange outer vertical edge = 5.0mm, and extrusion depth = 30.0mm.
